channel.title: BinderBrief Daily
channel.description: Daily commercial insurance and underwriting automation insights.

item.title: Canada wants 60% AI adoption – here's what that means for SMEs and cyber risk
item.description:
BOXX Canada is incorporating AI risk into its cyber insurance pricing despite not currently asking applicants about AI usage, in response to the Canadian government’s plan to increase AI adoption among SMEs from 12% to 60% with over $2 billion in investments. BOXX assumes AI adoption is widespread and factors risks such as data exfiltration, prompt injection, and AI-driven cyber threats into underwriting decisions, emphasizing the need for data governance and mapping before AI deployment. This approach aims to improve underwriting accuracy and risk management, preserving broker margins and carrier capacity as AI reshapes cyber risk exposures.
Name: Neal Jardine, Company: BOXX Canada, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: COO and President of BOXX Canada focusing on cyber insurance and AI risk management Name: Mark Carney, Company: Government of Canada, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Prime Minister of Canada who unveiled the AI adoption investment strategy

item.title: Sixfold introduces AI underwriter to support insurance underwriting decisions
item.description:
Sixfold, an AI solutions provider for insurance, announced the launch of its AI Underwriter platform following a $30 million Series B funding round. The platform uses a core "Underwriting Brain" intelligence that learns from each insurer’s unique underwriting appetite, portfolio data, and historical decisions to generate recommendations, explanations, and next steps during submission processing. This AI integration helps insurers reduce processing times by up to 97%, increase hit ratios by at least 15%, and grow gross written premiums per underwriter by up to 30%, improving underwriting efficiency and capacity.
Name: Taylor Mixides, Company: Reinsurance News, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article covering insurance industry news Name: Alex Schmelkin, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of Sixfold responsible for platform vision Name: Melissa Butt, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Vice President E&S Brokerage, Property, user of the AI Underwriter platform Name: John Burkhart, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President US Property & Casualty, client partner for Sixfold technology Name: Matthew Richardson, Company: Generali GC&C, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Operations & IT, user of Sixfold’s underwriting solutions Name: Jane Tran, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and COO of Sixfold, involved in product development and strategy

item.title: Tugboat Raises $3 Million Seed Round to Expand Homeowner Insurance Claims Platform
item.description:
Tugboat, an insurtech platform supporting homeowners with property insurance claims, raised $3 million in a seed funding round led by ResilienceVC and other investors. The platform uses agentic AI workflows informed by over 10,000 historical claims to guide homeowners through document review, denial disputes, and settlement negotiations for a $99 annual fee. This technology improves claims outcomes, helping users recover substantial additional settlements, thereby increasing loss recovery efficiency and expanding consumer support options amid rising climate-related disaster frequency.
Name: Cameron Mooney, Company: Tugboat, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and CEO of Tugboat focusing on homeowner insurance claims support Name: Aaron Mooney, Company: Tugboat, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Tugboat and former insurance adjuster Name: Tahira Dosani, Company: ResilienceVC, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and General Partner at ResilienceVC, lead investor in Tugboat Name: ResilienceVC, Company: ResilienceVC, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Venture capital firm investing in climate resilience and insurtech startups Name: South Dakota First, Company: South Dakota First, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Strategic investor in Tugboat's seed funding round Name: gener8tor, Company: gener8tor, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Startup accelerator and investor participating in Tugboat's seed round Name: Sure Ventures, Company: Sure Ventures, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Investment firm involved in Tugboat's seed funding Name: Acumen America, Company: Acumen America, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Investor focusing on innovative early-stage companies including Tugboat

item.title: New Report Reveals Half of Insurers Have Integrated AI into Core Functions
item.description:
Earnix released the "Insurance 2026: Earnix AI Trends Bulletin | UK Survey Results In Depth," showing that 55% of UK insurers have integrated AI into core functions such as pricing, underwriting, claims, and customer engagement, based on data from over 400 insurance executives globally including 40 UK leaders. The report details widespread adoption of AI with 98% of UK insurers using or planning to use generative AI for unstructured data processing, supported by increased investment in third-party data and a regulatory environment that moderately slows innovation. This integration improves carrier underwriting efficiency and operational workflows but reveals challenges in scaling AI solutions and meeting growing customer personalization expectations, affecting commercial broker margins and competitive capacity.
Name: Adrian Mincher, Company: Earnix, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of UK, Ireland and South Africa at Earnix who commented on AI integration trends in insurance

item.title: "Only an insurance product built with AI can truly insure AI"
item.description:
Lemonade announced that 98% of its insurance policies are sold using artificial intelligence and that over 60% of claims are processed through AI, with a recent product collaboration to insure Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software. The company integrates AI across policy underwriting, claims handling, coding, HR, and finance, using telematics and real-time software updates to dynamically price risk based on behavior and software versions. This AI-driven approach reduces operational costs, improves underwriting accuracy, and introduces new capacity for insuring AI-driven autonomous vehicles, impacting broker margins and carrier efficiency.
Name: Adina Eckstein, Company: Lemonade, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: COO of Lemonade responsible for leveraging AI across insurance operations

item.title: Cybersecurity Forum: Risks Up, Insurance Costs Down
item.description:
Cybersecurity risks for family offices are increasing due to more frequent and sophisticated attacks, with nearly half of US family offices targeted last year, yet cyber insurance premiums are becoming more competitive as firms like Chubb, AIG, and CNA enter the market. The threat landscape has expanded through AI-driven attack methods, social media data mining, and prolonged undetected intrusions, with family offices' informal structures creating vulnerabilities. This dynamic lowers underwriting costs and improves insurance capacity but requires brokers and carriers to adjust risk assessments and coverage offerings for emerging AI-related cyber threats.
Name: Mykolus Rambus, Company: Hush, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of a digital protection firm focusing on cybersecurity. Name: Scott Fogarty, Company: Ridgeback Network Defense, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO providing network defense and cybersecurity services. item.title: Sixfold Launches AI Underwriter -
item.description:
Sixfold has launched its AI Underwriter following a $30 million Series B funding round, designed to assist property and casualty insurers with automated underwriting. The platform uses an AI model called the Underwriting Brain, which integrates carrier-specific data, submission details, broker information, and underwriting guidelines to recommend next steps in underwriting submissions and can be configured for straight-through processing. This technology improves underwriting efficiency, leading to faster processing times, higher hit ratios, and increased gross written premiums per underwriter while maintaining human oversight.
Name: Alex Schmelkin, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of Sixfold Name: Melissa Butt, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Vice President E&S Brokerage, Property at Skyward Specialty Name: John Burkhart, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President US Property & Casualty at Skyward Specialty Name: Matthew Richardson, Company: Generali GC&C, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Operations & IT at Generali GC&C Name: Jane Tran, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and COO of Sixfold

item.title: Insurtech Evolves with Strategic Partnerships and Embedded Insurance
item.description:
InsurTech NY held its 2026 Spring Conference in New York City where industry leaders discussed the sector's shift from market disruption toward strategic partnerships and embedded insurance solutions. These developments involve integrating insurance into the customer experience through collaboration between insurers and technology providers, streamlining operations and adopting AI-driven innovations. The evolution increases underwriting efficiency and offers brokers new alternative risk management options, potentially impacting commercial broker margins and expanding carrier capacity.
Name: Ron Johnston, Company: Amwins, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Executive discussing public entity insurance market and parametric insurance as fiscal necessity. item.title: IntellectAI’s Magic Placement cuts quote workloads by 60%
item.description:
IntellectAI has launched Magic Placement, an AI-powered platform that reduces commercial insurance quote processing workloads by 60%. The platform uses a five-step AI pipeline to extract, structure, and validate unstructured quote documents into normalized JSON data, supporting integration via UI workflows and APIs. This technology cuts manual data entry by 60%, speeds up quote review by 50%, increases operational productivity by up to 40%, and reduces processing costs by about 30%, improving broker efficiency and underwriting turnaround times.
Name: N/A, Company: IntellectAI, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Provider of Magic Placement AI platform for insurance quote extraction and processing

item.title: Cyber incidents, economy and AI are top risks: Triple-I, Munich Re
item.description:
The Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I) and Munich Re released the RiskScan 2026 survey based on responses from over 1,700 participants, identifying cyber incidents, economic pressures, and AI as top insurance risks. The survey used online data collection by RTi Research to assess risk exposures in the U.S. and U.K., analyzing factors like inflation, geopolitical uncertainty, and supply chain issues as risk multipliers. These findings highlight increased challenges to insurer capital allocation, underwriting accuracy, and market stability, influencing commercial broker margins and carrier risk management strategies.
Name: Michel Léonard, Company: The Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief economist and data scientist at Triple-I Name: Sean Kevelighan, Company: The Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Triple-I Name: Kaitlyn Mattson, Company: Arizent, Email: kaitlyn.mattson@arizent.com, Type: Individual, Info: Managing editor of Digital Insurance and author of the article

item.title: Exclusive: Sixfold launches AI underwriting agent with straight-through quote and bind capability
item.description:
Sixfold announced the launch of its AI Underwriter, an AI agent designed to provide straight-through quote and bind capabilities for property and casualty insurers, supported by $30 million in Series B funding completed in January 2026. The AI Underwriter integrates decisions from underwriters into a proprietary institutional memory, maintaining strict data separation between carriers and allowing configurable autonomous processing up to quote-ready and bind-ready outputs. This technology decreases underwriting processing times by 50% to 97%, increases hit ratios by 15% or more, and raises gross written premium per underwriter by up to 30%, improving carrier underwriting efficiency and capacity.
Name: Alex Schmelkin, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of Sixfold, leading development of AI underwriting solutions. Name: Jane Tran, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Operating Officer and co-founder of Sixfold. Name: Brian Moseley, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Technology Officer and co-founder of Sixfold. Name: John Burkhart, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President of U.S. property and casualty at Skyward Specialty. Name: Matthew Richardson, Company: Generali Global Corporate & Commercial, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Operations and IT at Generali GC&C.

item.title: Online safety push is creating an insurance problem
item.description:
The UK government's online safety crackdown is shifting liability exposure from social media content to product design risks, affecting policies traditionally focused on media, privacy, and cyber coverage. The change involves legal claims centered on defective design, failure to warn, and regulatory compliance tied to platform and device architecture rather than user-generated content. This shift increases insurer exposure to product liability, recall risk, and regulatory fines, complicating underwriting and potentially reducing commercial broker margins on technology-related insurance products.
Name: Rosehana Amin, Company: Clyde & Co, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Partner at a London law firm specializing in technology and insurance law Name: Ed Ventham, Company: Assured Cyber, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of a specialist cyber and technology insurance broker in London Name: Keir Starmer, Company: UK Government, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: UK Prime Minister issuing regulatory demands on tech companies

item.title: Guillaume Bonnissent’s Insurance Technology Diary: Training pay
item.description:
Guillaume Bonnissent, CEO of Quotech, discussed the challenges of securing confidential data when using large language model (LLM) chatbots, including differences between so-called "enterprise" AI accounts and cloud platforms like AWS Bedrock. The article explains that Bedrock provides data processing within a customer’s cloud tenancy to prevent data from being used in AI training, while typical enterprise AI accounts may not guarantee data protection without reviewing contracts such as DPAs or BAAs. This distinction matters because secure data handling and jurisdictional control impact insurer and MGA compliance, operational risk, and potential data leakage that could affect underwriting efficiency and broker risk management.
Name: Guillaume Bonnissent, Company: Quotech, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Quotech and author of the Insurance Technology Diary

item.title: What 6,000 Insurance Leaders Were Actually Whispering About at Insurtech Insights USA
item.description:
ePayPolicy reported that over 6,000 insurance industry leaders convened at Insurtech Insights USA to discuss how legacy systems and AI-driven modernization are shaping the future of insurance. The event highlighted that insurers struggle with outdated infrastructure that limits integration and agility, while AI and connected APIs offer tools to automate workflows, improve underwriting precision, and enhance customer experience. These shifts affect commercial broker margins and carrier efficiency by emphasizing incremental modernization over large-scale projects and aligning technology upgrades with customer and operational needs.
Name: Josh Peterson, Company: ePayPolicy, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Product Officer at ePayPolicy and panelist on digital transformation in insurance Name: Tony Skipper, Company: Pinnacle Ridge Partners, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Managing Partner and Former CEO of Allianz Technology of America Name: Raj Kalahasthi, Company: Catalyx Advisory, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of Catalyx Advisory focusing on change management and transformation Name: Reuven Shnaps, Company: AmTrust Financial Services, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: SVP, Chief Data Science and AI Officer at AmTrust Financial Services Name: Evy Strom, Company: ePayPolicy, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author and attendee reporting on Insurtech Insights USA for ePayPolicy

item.title: AI linked to half of Australian cyber incidents, QBE
item.description:
QBE reported that 50% of Australian businesses experienced a cyber incident in the past year, with 26% of these involving artificial intelligence. The survey identified AI-driven attacks such as vulnerability identification, malware, phishing, deepfakes, and business email compromise, and showed that 66% of incidents were linked to third-party suppliers, highlighting interconnected risk exposure. This development increases cyber risk complexity, leading to greater demand for cyber insurance and driving higher IT security spending among commercial brokers and carriers to manage operational and supply chain vulnerabilities.
Name: Dominic Keller, Company: QBE, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Cyber Services at QBE responsible for cyber risk strategy and insights. Name: Mark Tarre, Company: SecurityBrief Australia, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: News Chief and author of the article on cybersecurity and AI risk trends.

item.title: Gallagher Re warns AI model evaluation must improve to underpin AI risk pricing
item.description:
Gallagher Re issued a report warning that current AI model evaluation methods are inadequate for underwriting AI risk, emphasizing the need for improved assessment focusing on model failures and correlated risks. The report critiques reliance on benchmark performance tests and highlights challenges from restricted-distribution models like Anthropic’s Mythos, which limit independent evaluation. This affects insurers’ ability to price AI risk accurately, potentially increasing uncertainty loadings, underwriting costs, and concentration risk in the commercial insurance market.
Name: Ed Pocock, Company: Gallagher Re, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global head of cyber security at Gallagher Re

item.title: How Upguys turned men’s health taboos into a $25-million business
item.description:
Upguys is a Vancouver-based men’s health telemedicine company generating $25 million in annual revenue with over 50,000 active patients across six Canadian provinces. The company integrates telemedicine consultations, laboratory testing covered by public health plans, and in-house pharmacy operations to deliver prescription-based treatments, particularly focusing on testosterone replacement therapy without monthly or per-appointment fees. This vertically integrated model enhances margins by controlling dispensing and compliance, improves patient retention, and provides accessible treatment options in underserviced areas, impacting commercial healthcare delivery and pharmacy margins.
Name: Mat Rezaei, Company: Upguys, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and pharmacist leading development of a men’s health telemedicine and pharmacy platform Name: Ramin Behzadi, Company: Upguys, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder focused on regulatory compliance and business scaling of Upguys Name: Rob Shaw, Company: BCBusiness, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Journalist and author of the article covering business and health sectors in British Columbia

item.title: The Top 15 AI Fintech, InsurTech & Compliance Scale-Ups You Need to Know in 2026
item.description:
Anzen raised $16 million in a Series A round to develop Anzen Pro, an AI-driven platform for specialty commercial insurance that restructures broker workflows by converting unstructured submissions into structured data and integrating with existing agency management systems. Avantos secured $35 million in total funding to build an AI-native financial operating system that uses a knowledge graph to unify client data across custodians, CRMs, underwriting, and policy tools, enabling AI agents to automate tasks without manual reconciliation. These platforms reduce overhead and improve operational throughput, increasing broker margins and enabling carriers and financial institutions to scale underwriting, distribution, and compliance workflows more efficiently.
Name: Max Bruner, Company: Anzen, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder of Anzen, a company focused on AI-powered specialty commercial insurance distribution. item.title: NTT DATA: Insurance Industry's US$700bn Cyber Risk Challenge
item.description:
NTT DATA’s Insurtech Global Outlook 2026 report projects uninsured cyber losses rising from US$171bn in 2023 to over US$700bn by 2030. The report highlights insurer challenges in scaling AI solutions due to governance and trust issues despite AI delivering up to 35% cost savings and increasing demand for prevention-led services. This accelerating cyber risk and slow AI adoption pressure insurers to improve underwriting efficiency and shift toward continuous risk detection to protect margins and capacity.
Name: Bruno Abril, Company: NTT DATA, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Insurance responsible for insurance industry strategy at NTT DATA

item.title: Cyber, Catastrophe, and AI Top Risk Concerns as Protection Gaps Persist, Survey Finds
item.description:
The State of Vermont appointed Christine Brown as Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance in 2026, overseeing a leading onshore captive insurance domicile with over 1,200 licensed captives. Vermont's captive division employs extensive industry expertise and is modernizing technology systems, including exploring AI tools, to improve regulatory responsiveness and efficiency. This approach supports stable regulation that sustains captive owner success, enhances market confidence, and helps maintain Vermont’s competitive position in captive insurance.
Name: Christine Brown, Company: State of Vermont, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Deputy Commissioner of Captive Insurance leading Vermont’s captive insurance division Name: Kaj Samsom, Company: State of Vermont, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Commissioner of the Department of Financial Regulation supporting the captive insurance division
